input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In .NET 10 (and actually starting with some .NET 9 previews), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ in favor of the new `WindowInsets` API. MAUI has been transitioning to edge-to-edge rendering and the new insets-based approach.


Android officially deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` at API 30, and .NET MAUI has shifted toward edge-to-edge display support in .NET 10, moving away from the older resize-based approach.

For practical implementation, MAUI provides `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which handles most ScrollView scenarios automatically, but for custom layouts like a chat screen with a bottom input bar, I need to listen to keyboard insets and adjust padding accordingly rather than relying on the deprecated resize mode. Setting the keyboard adjustment mode...
